name: Polyvinyl Chloride Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Polyvinyl Chloride. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for PVC cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal PVC absorption and minimal substrate damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise PVC cleaning

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient PVC removal

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective PVC ablation

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled PVC ablation

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform PVC removal

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete PVC removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform PVC cleaning

power.description: Typical range for polymer cleaning to avoid thermal damage; starting at 50 W for PVC's low melting point (~160°C); max safe ~150 W to prevent substrate melting. Source: IPG Photonics specs for fiber lasers in polymer applications; studies in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on PVC ablation.

scan_speed.description: Effective for contamination removal without overheating; slower (500-1000 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (2000-3000 mm/s) for light; recommended 1000 mm/s balances efficiency and safety for PVC's poor thermal conductivity. Source: Industry standards from Laser Cleaning Europe guidelines; case studies in Journal of Cleaner Production (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for industrial cleaning of polymers to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond for ultra-precision but costlier; 30 ns optimal for PVC to enable photothermal ablation without excessive melting. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) on polymer thresholds; avoids fs/ps due to equipment availability.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning; 20-50 kHz recommended for PVC to achieve fast processing while limiting heat accumulation from low reflectivity (~5-10% at 1064 nm); higher rates increase speed but risk thermal buildup. Source: Academic literature in Optics Express (2021) on repetition rate effects in polymer laser cleaning.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe ablation of contaminants on polymers; PVC damage threshold ~0.5 J/cm²; recommended 0.3 J/cm² for cleaning without substrate degradation, considering low thermal conductivity (0.19 W/mK). Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2017) for PVC at 1064 nm.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for effective coverage; smaller (50-100 μm) for fine details, larger (200-300 μm) for broad areas; 150 μm recommended for uniform PVC surface preparation without pinpoint damage. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems for industrial cleaning.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-4) for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete PVC cleaning to ensure removal without cumulative heat damage. Source: Industry practice in case studies from Surface and Coatings Technology (2022); avoids excessive passes due to PVC's heat sensitivity.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without gaps or over-ablation; 50% optimal for PVC to prevent uneven heating and maintain surface integrity during scanning. Source: Process optimization studies in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2020); typical 30-80% range for polymer applications.
